etymology.description: semantic ⺼ (meat, body) + phonetic 卻 (sound) -> part of the body, the foot or leg
etymology.interpretation: The character 腳(腳) (jiǎo, "foot") combines the semantic component ⺼(肉) (ròu, "meat, body") which indicates a part of the body, with the phonetic component 卻(卻) (què) to suggest the sound. This blend points to a specific body part, here denoting the foot or leg.

examples.chinese: 我的腳很痛(我的腳很痛)。
他的腳比我的大(他的腳比我的大)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de jiǎo bǐ wǒ de dà.
examples.english: His foot is bigger than mine.

examples.chinese: 她踢足球的时候受伤了她的腳(她踢足球的時候受傷了她的腳)。
examples.pinyin: Tā tī zúqiú de shíhòu shòushāngle tā de jiǎo.
examples.english: She injured her foot while playing soccer.
